content.rendered:
Herbert Holler of the legendary Freedom Party® presents Jams, a new late night party celebrating the best of late 90’s + 2000’s R&B, pop, and hip-hop with classics and deep cuts from TLC, 112, Aaliyah, Jagged Edge, Justin Timberlake, Destiny’s Child, and more. Hosted by Savior Elmundo.

BIRTHDAY SPECIAL: June bdays free when you email full name, bday and “Jams NYC” to herbert@herbertholler.com (while supplies last!)

content.rendered:
We’re conjuring up something magical 🪄⚡️ Join us on Saturday, June 6th for Wizard Rave, a night of pure chaos, spells, and nonstop dancing to EDM remixes and pop bangers all night long.

Calling all Gryffindors, Slytherins, Ravenclaws, and Hufflepuffs… it’s time to report to the dance floor. Expect high energy, questionable decisions, and a little bit of dark magic in the air.

See you on the dance floor 👀✨

content.rendered:
Heartworm and LPR present: For the first time ever, an immersive live performance of Cold Cave’s Cremations era material. Misanthropic synth hymnals and icy electronic glory, once described as Whitehouse meets The Cure.

Acclaimed artist and singer Rosa Anschütz performs material from her epic album Sabbatical, one year after its release.

These live rituals take place September 11 in Los Angeles at Hollywood Forever Cemetery and September 19 in Brooklyn at First Unitarian Church.

content.rendered:
New York Choir Project (or NYCP for those who love a good acronym) is a collection of professionally led amateur choirs who turn pop and rock hits (and some sleepers) into a 5-part, joyous wall of sound – like some crazy musical alchemy. From Bowie to Beyoncé, the Cranberries to Chappel Roan,* you’re bound to find yourself dancing, singing along, reliving your youth…you’ll dig it, is what we’re saying.

*Artists we’ve covered, not a set list spoiler!

7 p.m. Metropop Choir – young, fresh and foot-tapping tunes

7:30 p.m. B-Side Choir – current grooves meet flip-side classics

8:20 p.m. Brooklynite Choir – joyous noise with a hint of indie rock

8:50 p.m. Britpop Choir – bloody-brilliant across-the-pond harmonies
